calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Jan-03-2022 Vacated

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Discovery Responses From Defendant John H. Bailey Ii
Off Calendar Aug-23-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Charlie Chou, Esq. (415-795-3579; cchou@astralegal.com) of Ad Astra Law Group, LLP for Plaintiff.
John Bailey (510-778-6547; jhbii007@gmail.com), Defendant in propria persona.

THE PARTIES STIPULATE TO THE MATTER BEING DECIDED BY THE JUDGE PRO TEM. THE PARTIES AND THE JUDGE PRO TEM APPEARING REMOTELY AUTHORIZE THE CLERK TO SIGN THE FORMAL STIPULATION FORM ON THEIR BEHALF.

ARGUED. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PLAINTIFF JAMES NURMI's Motion To Compel Further Discovery Responses From Defendant John H. Bailey II:

Motion granted. Defendant shall provide supplemental, verified responses and produce responsive documents by September 20, 2021. For the RFPDs in which Defendant agreed to produce all responsive, non privileged documents (i.e. RFPD Nos. 1, 3, 5, 8, 10-33) he need only submit a verification for those responses. For all other requests where no response was made, the objections are overruled and a response is due.

Moving party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312 via email to davididaho14@gmail.com.

Judge Pro Tem: David McDonald, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Balinda Dunlap, CSR # 10710, (916-216-1838; balindadunlap@yahoo.com). =(302/JPT)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Subpoena
Off Calendar Aug-03-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. CHARLIE Y. CHOU, Esq. of Ad Astra Law Group, LLP (Tel: 415-795-3579) for plaintiff James Douglas Nurmi;
Defendant JOHN H. BAILEY II (Tel: 510-778-6547) in pro per.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT JOHN BAILEY II's Motion To Quash Subpoena.

Both sides stipulate to the matter being decided by the Judge Pro Tem. The Judge Pro Tem and both sides all appearing remotely authorize the clerk to sign the formal stipulation form on their behalf.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

The motion to quash is granted to the extent that the subpoena to Bank of America is limited to (1) cash transactions and (2) payments, deposits and/or transfers to or from James Douglas Nurmi, John H. Bailey, John Drago, Jonathan Lupo, Mr. Doll, and/or Thorium Cyber Security, LLC. Moving party has on July 31, 2021, one court day before the hearing, submitted to the Temporary Judge five largely identical documents, each with eight apparently identical exhibits. The documents are not conformed with a filing stamp or accompanied by a proof of service. The documents are obviously inappropriate and untimely and will not be considered. The motion is otherwise denied.

Order signed.

Judge Pro Tem: James Fleming; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/JPT)
